1: In thee, O LORD LORD , do I put my trust: Let me never be put to confusion.
2: Deliver me in thy righteousness, and cause me to escape: Incline thine ear unto me, and save me.
3: Thou hast given commandment to save me; For thou art art my rock and my fortress.
4: Out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
5: Thou art Thou art my trust from my youth.
6: Thou art he that took me out of my mother's bowels: My praise shall be shall be continually of thee.
7: But thou art art my strong refuge.
8: And with And with thy honour all the day.
9: Forsake me not when my strength faileth.
10: And they that lay wait for my soul take counsel together,
11: God hath forsaken him: Persecute and take him; for there is there is none to deliver him him .
12: O my God, make haste for my help.
13: Let them be covered with with reproach and dishonour that seek my hurt.
14: And will yet praise thee more and more.
15: For I know not the numbers thereof thereof .
16: I will make mention of thy righteousness, even even of thine only.
17: And hitherto have I declared thy wondrous works.
18: Until I have shewed thy strength unto this this generation, and and thy power to every one that that is to come.
19: Who hast done great things: O God, who is is like unto thee!
20: And shalt bring me up again from the depths of the earth.
21: And comfort me on every side.
22: Unto thee will I sing with the harp, O thou Holy One of Israel.
23: And my soul, which thou hast redeemed.
24: For they are confounded, for they are brought unto shame, that seek my hurt.
